name: House of the Dragon
comment: The scene with the stag absolutely wrecked me. I couldn't watch it at all. I covered my eyes and ears, and my bf turned the volume way down. I still ended up crying after the scene, and bf was affected as well. He didn't turn down the volume specifically for me, it was already pretty quiet, and I covered my eyes. But he all but muted the tv during that scene because of how bad it was. Though you absolutely have time to prepare and know when to skip, it's not from one scene to another it happens.

name: Chernobyl
comment: Unfortunately, Glaxona13, the Chernobyl liquidators did shoot healthy companion animals, under the rationale that it was necessary to stop the spread of radiation. In fact, there’s a story from one of the liquidators in Svetlana Alexievich’s oral history of the disaster, Voices from Chernobyl, that’s significantly more upsetting than what the series shows. In the companion Chernobyl podcast, they even discuss that they shot the more upsetting story but decided that showing it would be gratuitous, so they cut it. But the liquidators did not kill all the pet dogs, and their descendants still live in Chernobyl today! You can help a group that takes care of them, or just see cute pictures of them : https://cleanfutures.org/projects/dogs-of-chernobyl/. Before covid, some of them were even being adopted by US families! https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.buzzfeednews.com/amphtml/janelytvynenko/rescue-dogs-chernobyl

name: Red Dwarf
comment: A humanoid cat dies on-screen in "Waiting For God". The same episode also refers to multiple off-screen deaths among the cat people, including their killing one another in religious disputes, a group of cats flying directly into an asteroid, and the sick and injured being abandoned to die. Lister's pet cat, Frankenstein (who appears in "The End"), passes on during his time in stasis, but her death is not depicted on-screen.
